_about: Synthetic task fixtures for the memory projection HONESTY HARNESS (Tier 1). ALL DATA IS FICTIONAL. No real names, paths, keys, or private facts. Each task labels a hand-picked GOLD set: 'required' = entries a good answer needs (graded for recall), 'pin' = a non-negotiable that must survive projection at any budget (safety/identity/operational), 'noise' = a deliberate distractor that competes for budget. Required entries are written so the lexical query proxy CAN surface them, but several are dated/episodic so they score low on static intrinsic value — that gap is the honest point: static projection under-serves task recall, query-aware projection helps, and remaining misses are reported, not hidden. See skills/memory-honesty-harness.md.

tasks._note: RECOVERY case: the two required entries are dated/episodic (low static score), so static projection drops BOTH (0% recall) while query-aware projection surfaces them (100%). The clearest demonstration that query-awareness recovers episodic recall the static fallback cannot.
tasks.query: the telegram poller keeps dropping events again, how do I recover the watchdog

memory.text: Long-form notes live in `~/.hermes/notes/INDEX.md`. Read INDEX.md first to find any long-form topic.
memory.label: notes-header

memory.text: Root cause (2026-02-10): the telegram poller dropped events because an unawaited coroutine swallowed them; fix is to await the handler and add a backpressure queue.
memory.label: telegram-poller-rootcause

memory.text: Watchdog recovery procedure: stop the gateway, pull latest, restart the watchdog, then verify the poller heartbeat before walking away.
memory.label: watchdog-recovery

memory.text: Root cause (2026-01-22): the semantic daemon OOMed when the embedding batch size was left unbounded; cap the batch to fix it.
memory.label: noise-semantic-oom

memory.text: Cron scheduler fixed on 2026-01-30: duplicate fires came from two enabled schedules sharing one id, deployed and verified.
memory.label: noise-cron-dup

memory.text: NCLEX pharmacology cards need a high-yield rationale and a side-effect cluster per drug class.
memory.label: noise-nclex

memory.text: Trading POI identification uses order blocks and liquidity inducement; the spec doc is the source of truth.
memory.label: noise-trading

user.text: User prefers blunt, ROI-focused correction over reassurance, always.
user.label: noise-pref-blunt

user.text: User wants a brief plan then immediate action when the next step is obvious.
user.label: noise-pref-action

tasks._note: HARD case (weak overlap): the error-journal required entry shares few words with the query, so the weak lexical proxy under-ranks it and it is squeezed out -> partial recall (WARN). An honest demonstration that the Tier-1 proxy misses weakly-overlapping required context; the real semantic model should do better (Tier 2 confirms).

tasks._note: HARD case (high-value off-topic competition): two safety pins survive, but at this budget several cheap high-static off-topic entries out-score one required entry on the blended score, so projection keeps only one of the two required -> partial recall (WARN). Honest signal that relevance (weight 0.20) can lose to off-topic static value at tight budgets.

tasks._note: POSITIVE CONTROL: noise is genuinely low-value (dated status / vague) and the required entries are concise. Projection KEEPS both required design entries in BOTH static and query-aware modes — the positive half of the savings-without-loss claim (it works when competing entries are low-value).

tasks._note: SAFETY case: three safety pins must survive projection (verified at budget=0), AND the incident runbook must be recalled. Budget is set above pins+required so we test SELECTION, not arithmetic impossibility (that case is covered by a unit test).
tasks.query: an api key may have leaked in a log, what is the incident procedure to rotate and audit

memory.text: API key policy: keys are for the gateway service only; never paste an API key into task work, logs, or commits.
memory.label: apikey-policy-pin

memory.text: Never share API key tokens or other secrets in logs, screenshots, or commit messages.
memory.label: never-share-secrets-pin

memory.text: Incident runbook for a leaked key: rotate the key via the ops console first, then audit the access logs for misuse, then re-issue to the gateway.
memory.label: leaked-key-runbook
